surface.name: Query and visualize data from a notebook
surface.selection_basis: The Databricks Get started index lists Data exploration first and places Query and visualize data first within that section. The Free Edition sign-up page also lists this tutorial first among resources for a new workspace, so it is the strongest vendor-selected general first-use route.

alternatives_considered.name: Create your first table and grant privileges
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Free Edition page lists it after Query and visualize data, and the main index places it third in Data exploration.

alternatives_considered.name: Build an ETL pipeline
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a data-engineering-specific tutorial rather than the first item in the general index.

alternatives_considered.name: Query LLMs and prototype AI agents
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is an AI-specific branch listed after general data exploration tutorials.

entry_point.developer_intent: Use a Databricks notebook to query the built-in NYC taxi sample table and save a bar-chart visualization of the results.
assumed_prior_state: The developer does not yet have a Databricks workspace.
The developer is eligible to use Free Edition for learning or experimentation, or has equivalent workspace access.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Complete Tutorial: Query and visualize data from a notebook.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A new Databricks notebook queries samples.nyctaxi.trips and contains a saved bar chart configured from the query results.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: After the developer clicks Save in the visualization editor, the configured bar chart appears as a notebook output visualization.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The tutorial's final numbered action is Click Save after configuring the bar chart; the page then moves to Next steps.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Save action completes the last numbered step of the first tutorial selected by both the getting-started index and the new-workspace resources. Additional ingestion, transformation, dashboard, and ML tutorials are explicitly next steps.

prerequisites.requirement: A Databricks account and workspace; Free Edition is the no-payment path for personal learning and experimentation.
A workspace with Unity Catalog enabled.
Permission to use an existing compute resource or create one; a Free Edition workspace includes serverless compute.

primary_path.action: Open the Databricks Get started tutorials and select Query and visualize data.
details: It is the first tutorial under Data exploration.
primary_path.input: Intent to perform a first hands-on Databricks task.
primary_path.success_signal: The tutorial requirements and Step 1 are visible.

primary_path.action: Sign up for Databricks Free Edition or obtain access to an eligible workspace.
details: For Free Edition, go to the sign-up page and choose a preferred sign-up method.
Databricks creates a new serverless workspace with default storage.
primary_path.input: Supported personal sign-up identity or organizational workspace invitation.
primary_path.success_signal: The new workspace opens and serverless compute is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Free Edition is quota-limited and intended for learning, training, and non-commercial use; business evaluation follows the free-trial branch.

primary_path.action: Click New in the workspace sidebar, then click Notebook.
primary_path.success_signal: A blank notebook opens in the workspace.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing workspace or compute permissions can block notebook execution later.

primary_path.action: Paste one vendor-provided query for samples.nyctaxi.trips into the empty cell.
details: The tutorial offers SQL, Python, Scala, and R versions.
Free Edition supports the SQL or Python choices used by this sample path.
primary_path.input: One language-specific query from the tutorial.
primary_path.output: Notebook cell containing a sample-table query.
primary_path.success_signal: The cell contains a syntactically complete query.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Language availability depends on workspace type and compute.

primary_path.action: Press Shift+Enter to run the cell.
primary_path.output: Executed query against Unity Catalog sample data.
primary_path.success_signal: Query results appear in the notebook and focus moves to the next cell.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unity Catalog, sample-catalog access, compute permission, or quota issues can prevent results.

primary_path.action: Click the plus control next to Table and select Visualization.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The option is unavailable without a compatible result table.

primary_path.action: Configure the chart as Bar with fare_amount on X and trip_distance on Y.
primary_path.input: Visualization editor and taxi result columns.
primary_path.success_signal: The editor preview reflects the selected axes.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Incorrect columns produce a different visualization.

primary_path.action: Set aggregation to Average and Group by to pickup_zip.
primary_path.output: Average trip-distance visualization grouped by pickup ZIP code.
primary_path.success_signal: The preview matches the tutorial image and grouping.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Different aggregation or grouping changes the tutorial outcome.

primary_path.action: Click Save in the visualization editor.
primary_path.success_signal: The chart is retained as a visualization tab in the notebook output, completing the tutorial.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Leaving without saving does not preserve the configured visualization.

branches.condition: The developer is evaluating Databricks for an organization or needs the full platform.
branches.path: Choose the free trial instead of Free Edition and complete its cloud/account onboarding.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds commercial account, cloud, and possibly payment gates before the same notebook tutorial.

branches.condition: The developer chooses the tutorial's Genie Code Agent mode shortcut.
branches.path: Give Genie Code the supplied prompt to create a notebook, query the sample table, and display the requested bar chart.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Agent mode can generate the notebook and visualization instead of the manual numbered route.

branches.condition: The developer prefers a language other than SQL.
branches.path: Use the documented Python, Scala, or R sample-table query where supported by the workspace.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Only the notebook code changes; query results and visualization steps remain the same.

branches.condition: No eligible compute resource is attached or usable.
branches.path: Select an existing resource or create one if permitted; Free Edition uses its included serverless compute.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a compute-selection or permission gate before the query runs.

friction_gates.description: Workspace access is required, and the choice between Free Edition and free trial depends on intended use.
Free Edition requires no payment, while the business free trial can require a credit card for paid continuation.
The workspace must have Unity Catalog and the developer must be allowed to use or create compute.
Free Edition compute is subject to daily fair-usage quotas and can be unavailable after the quota is exceeded.

sources.title: Get started tutorials on Databricks
evidence_supported: Placement of Query and visualize data as first data-exploration tutorial

sources.title: Sign up for Databricks
sections_used: Choose the right option for you
evidence_supported: Free trial versus Free Edition decision
Identity, billing, commercial-use, and feature gates

sources.title: Sign up for Databricks Free Edition
Tutorial: Query and visualize data from a notebook

uncertainties.question: Which exact registration and verification screens appear for each current Free Edition sign-up method?
uncertainties.impact: Account creation is a documented gate but cannot be decomposed into all screen-level actions.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official page says to choose a preferred signup method, but does not enumerate method-specific screens.

uncertainties.question: Does a new Free Edition workspace always expose the tutorial sample catalog immediately under current quota conditions?
uncertainties.impact: A capacity or entitlement edge case could add a wait before the first query.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The tutorial states the catalog requirement and the Free Edition page states included compute, but neither guarantees immediate regional capacity.

excluded_after_success: Production security, networking, governance, and billing configuration
